definition: Stock-based compensation plus restructuring charges create a double-count quality problem where economic labor cost never appears in non-GAAP earnings at any stage of the hire-hoard-restructure lifecycle. Tech-sector SBC runs at 8-15% of revenue with GAAP-to-non-GAAP gaps of 20-35% for several mega-caps.
evidence: Lifecycle: (a) hire with SBC excluded from non-GAAP; (b) hoard with continued SBC exclusion; (c) restructure with severance excluded
Tech-sector SBC at 8-15% of revenue; GAAP-to-non-GAAP gaps of 20-35%
Meta example: $4.2B restructuring charge excluded from non-GAAP following 2020-2022 hiring surge
KB's $14-16/share SBC adjustment is steady-state and doesn't capture restructuring cycle increment
open_questions: Does the double-count accumulate over cycles or wash out?
What is the correct SBC adjustment for S&P EPS including the restructuring cycle increment?
How does this interact with buyback-SBC offset analysis?
